message:
fix(core): replace inline truncate logic with btrfs_remove_overlapping_extent_data helper

- Remove verbose debug logging from lookup_name failure path; use
  idiomatic `let-else` instead of match+eprintln cascade.
- Replace manual extent iteration/deletion in btrfs_setattr truncate
  with a call to the existing btrfs_remove_overlapping_extent_data
  helper, which correctly trims partially-overlapping extents rather
  than only deleting those fully beyond new EOF.
- Recompute inode.nbytes via btrfs_recompute_inode_nbytes after
  truncation instead of naively assigning inode.nbytes = size, which
  was incorrect for sparse files.
- Accept `cx` parameter in btrfs_setattr (was previously unused `_cx`).
- Add test btrfs_write_truncate_then_sparse_extend_zero_fills_gap
  proving the gap between truncated EOF and a later sparse write reads
  as zeros, not stale pre-truncate data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
